mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) over a 6-month period signals rising demand and inflation expectations, leading to higher S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in high-inflation periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% decrease in the unemployment rate over a 3-month period signals a tightening labor market, leading to higher S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in low-unemployment periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Industrial Production Index (INDPRO) over a 6-month period signals rising supply and economic growth, leading to higher S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in high-growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in the Housing Starts Index (HOUST) over a 3-month period signals rising demand and economic growth, leading to higher S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in high-growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1% decrease in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) and a 0.5% increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) over a 3-month period signal a monetary easing cycle and rising demand, leading to higher S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% decrease in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) and a 1% increase in the Nonfarm Payrolls Index (PAYEMS) over a 3-month period signal a tightening labor market and rising demand, leading to higher S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in low-unemployment periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Real Disposable Personal Income Index (RSAFS) over a 6-month period signals rising demand and economic growth, leading to higher S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in high-growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
